etymology.description: śąą(śąą) (gńď, "spear") + śąą(śąą) (gńď, "spear") = two spears facing each other -> original form of śģė(śģė) (c√°n, "to damage")
etymology.interpretation: The character śąĒ originally depicted two spears opposing each other, symbolizing conflict or damage, and evolved to represent the concept of reducing or damaging. This is also the original form of the character for 'to damage'.
